interactions.final_answer:
Thanks—I've read the IKEA profile carefully. Below is a **complete, build-ready design** (not a skeleton) covering scope config, 4–5 channels, hub-level polish, content calendar with real IKEA-specific themes/facts, FAQs, sticky policies, and planned homepage highlights. Before I start building anything: **no tool calls yet**. Please review and approve. --- ## PHASE 0 — DESIGN PROPOSAL (Complete Hub Design) ### 1) SCOPE CONFIG (Workspace) **Scope name:** IKEA Intranet (Co-workers) **Scope description:**

An intranet for IKEA co-workers to find practical, timely information—business updates, people & culture, and policy guidance—written in the IKEA way.

**Welcome message:**

Welcome, co-worker. Here you’ll find “IKEA Inside” updates, practical policies you can rely on, and the things you need to do your best work—with a little less complexity and a little more clarity.

**Scope hero brand logo (to be uploaded in Phase 1.5):** Logo.dev URL from your profile: https://img.logo.dev/ikea.com?token=pk_OwST1E04QH-w3oM0YksM2Q&retina=true **Theme colors (Diggspace mapping from profile):** - primary_color: **#0058AB** (IKEA Blue) - primary_color_contrast: **#FFFFFF** - secondary_color: **#FBD914** (IKEA Yellow) - warn_color: **#E00751** (Alert Red) - warn_color_contrast: **#FFFFFF** **Highlights rendering:** - number_of_highlights: **4** - highlights_carousel: **true** - highlights_style: **BannerCarousel** **Footer (3 columns, real links from profile):** 1. **About IKEA** - About/global corporate site: https://about.ikea.com - Careers: https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ - IKEA Foundation: https://www.ikeafoundation.org 2. **Investor & Performance** - Ingka annual summary: https://www.ingka.com/annual-summary-and-sustainability-report/ - Inter IKEA performance: https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/performance/ 3. **Support & Sustainability** - Sustainability hub: https://www.ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/ - IKEA Foundation (climate / children’s futures): https://www.ikeafoundation.org **Social links (from profile, global official):** - LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/ikea/ - Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/ikea/ - YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/ikea - X/Twitter: https://twitter.com/IKEA **navigation_links:** - **Topbarlinks (3)** - “IKEA Inside” → https://www.ingka.com/newsroom/ *(profile has Newsroom URLs; see note below)* - “Sustainability” → https://www.ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/ - “Work with us” → https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ - **Footerlinks (5)** - https://about.ikea.com - https://www.ingka.com/annual-summary-and-sustainability-report/ - https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/performance/ - https://www.ikeafoundation.org - https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ - **socialLinks**: LinkedIn/Instagram/YouTube/X (as above) > Quick note: your profile lists **two newsroom URLs** (Ingka and Inter IKEA). I will use the **more general Ingka Newsroom** if you want “IKEA Inside” phrased as Ingka-group news; otherwise tell me which newsroom link should be used in the topbar. **components (pick 1–3 based on profile):** - upcomingEvents - birthdays - workAnniversaries --- ### 2) CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE (5 channels) I’m using IKEA communication formats and co-worker reality: - primary channels = most day-to-day publishing + pinned items (sticky) - secondary channels = utility/policy/knowledge with fewer items + FAQ where it helps #### Ch1 (PRIMARY) — “IKEA Inside: Co-worker Updates” - **Purpose:** Timely business updates and operational signals, written like IKEA Inside. - **Type:** public - **Color:** **#0058AB** (brand primary) - **is_sticky:** True - **image_url:** **stock/financial_report.png** (financial reporting) - **FAQ (2–3):** 1) **Q:** “Where do I find the latest leadership transitions and what changed?” **A:**

Use this channel for CEO and Group changes. Look for items pinned to the top first—then read the linked context sections.

2) **Q:** “How do I interpret price reductions vs profit changes?” **A:**

We share the “what” and the “why” in plain language: FY24–FY25 price reductions, revenue movement, and what recovered in net profit.

3) **Q:** “Are updates global or country-specific?” **A:**

These are global updates. Local implications are shared by your region/store leadership in the same week.

- **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description (HTML, 2–3 sentences):**

For the things co-workers need to know—quickly and clearly. IKEA Inside items connect performance, product launches, and real-world decisions.

You’ll find pinned context first, then the details you can act on.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'Reader' **Target content (primary): 3 items** #### Ch2 (PRIMARY) — “People & Belonging: Tack! + Culture” - **Purpose:** People programs, culture rituals, learning, and co-worker wellbeing—without HR jargon. - **Type:** public - **Color:** **#FBD914** (warm tone / yellow accent) - **is_sticky:** True - **image_url:** **stock/team_diversity.png** (culture & belonging) - **FAQ:** 1) **Q:** “What is Tack! and how do I participate?” **A:**

Tack! is the internal thanks programme. Share a real “thank you” tied to something co-workers actually did.

2) **Q:** “How does hybrid work work here?” **A:**

Office co-workers operate on hybrid schedules. If your role requires on-site coverage, your local manager will clarify the model.

3) **Q:** “Where do learning opportunities live?” **A:**

Use the learning-related updates and link out to the internal training platform details published in this channel.

- **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description:**

Culture at IKEA isn’t a poster—it’s the way we solve problems together. This channel brings you the practical parts of “the IKEA way”: people programmes, learning, and wellbeing.

Look for pinned posts when something affects how we work.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'Reader' **Target content (primary): 3 items** #### Ch3 (PRIMARY) — “Democratic Design: Product, Price & Innovation” - **Purpose:** Product pipeline updates, launch recaps, and the “Democratic Design” cost-first story. - **Type:** public - **Color:** **#0058AB** - **is_sticky:** True - **image_url:** **stock/Innovation.png** (innovation) - **FAQ:** 1) **Q:** “What does Democratic Design mean for pricing?” **A:**

Design starts with the price target. If affordability fails, the product fails—no matter how elegant it looks.

2) **Q:** “Where do second-hand and circular initiatives fit?” **A:**

They’re part of the sustainability work and also capture value from the secondary market—starting with Sweden plans.

3) **Q:** “How do city-format stores change what we do?” **A:**

City formats change formats, assortment, and planning services—faster and more cost-efficient, while keeping co-worker and customer experience in focus.

- **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description:**

From Älmhult designers to store shelves: this is where we explain the “why” behind products and launches. You’ll see how we balance form, function, quality, sustainability, and low price.

When we make price reductions or launch a new format, this channel gives you the context.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'Reader' **Target content (primary): 3 items** --- #### Ch4 (SECONDARY) — “Security & IT Safety” - **Purpose:** Security guidance and IT best practices (co-worker responsibilities included). - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** **#0071BC** (cool neutral / IT accent) - **is_sticky:** False (secondary) - **image_url:** **stock/Password.png** - **FAQ (2–3):** 1) **Q:** “What do I do if I suspect a phishing message?” **A:**

Stop, do not click. Report it using your local IT reporting channel (follow the steps in this hub’s pinned Security items).

2) **Q:** “How should I handle sensitive files when working hybrid?” **A:**

Use approved systems only and avoid storing sensitive data locally. This keeps access controlled and traceable.

3) **Q:** “Why are password practices so strict?” **A:**

Because account compromise is preventable. Password hygiene is a shared protection, not “just IT rules.”

- **hide_on_homepage_feed:** True - **hide_highlights:** True - **Description:**

Security is not optional here. This channel contains IT safety guidance and policy items you must acknowledge before continuing with specific internal workflows.

Read carefully—short, clear steps beat long debates.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'None' **Target content (secondary): 2 items** (both require read confirmation) #### Ch5 (SECONDARY) — “Policies & Compliance Acknowledgements” - **Purpose:** Policies, legal/HR compliance acknowledgements, and “must-read” updates. - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** **#E00751** (warn/alert) - **is_sticky:** False (secondary) - **image_url:** **stock/document_review.png** - **FAQ (2–3):** 1) **Q:** “Do I need to acknowledge policy updates every time?” **A:**

Only items marked as required acknowledgements in this channel. Those will include ‘read confirmation’.

2) **Q:** “Which policies relate to working models and reorganisations?” **A:**

We publish the relevant policy consequences alongside restructure updates—so co-workers know what applies where.

3) **Q:** “Where can I find the official rationale and effective dates?” **A:**

Each policy item includes an effective date and a short rationale so you can understand the “why,” not just the “what.”

- **hide_on_homepage_feed:** True - **hide_highlights:** True - **Description:**

Policy items live here with clear effective dates and co-worker-readable language. When you see a policy with “read confirmation,” that means acknowledgement is required.

We keep the structure simple: read, confirm, and move on.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'None' **Target content (secondary): 2 items** (both require read confirmation) --- ### 3) CONTENT CALENDAR (by channel) I’m selecting **exactly 3–4 hub highlights total** via **is_main_highlight=True** on the content items themselves. #### Ch1 (PRIMARY) — IKEA Inside: Co-worker Updates (3 items) **Item 1 (Sticky + Highlight):** - Title: “FY25 in numbers: Ingka retail €41.5B revenue, net profit €1.4B (+75%)” - Type: recap - tags: ['fy25', 'financial-results', 'ingka', 'co-worker-update'] - is_main_highlight: **True** - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/financial_report.png **Body outline (what it covers):** Connect FY25 Ingka retail sales (€44.6B total IKEA retail), Ingka €41.5B revenue, net profit €1.4B (+75%), and the “why” (price reductions + normalized logistics). Ends with “Look for your local implications in the next week’s store/region briefings.” **Item 2 (Sticky):** - Title: “Leadership handover: Juvencio Maeztu (Ingka) and Jakub Jankowski (Inter IKEA)” - Type: announcement - tags: ['leadership', 'ingka', 'inter-ikea', '2026'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/leadership_speech.png **Body outline:** Summarize CEO changes (Maeztu since Nov 2025; Jankowski since Jan 2026), the “continuity of culture” note, and what co-workers should watch for: commercial urgency + simpler decision flows. **Item 3:** - Title: “Oxford Street opened in May 2025: what the city-format prototype changes for stores” - Type: spotlight - tags: ['oxford-street', 'store-format', 'customer', 'urban-strategy'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/office.png **Body outline:** Cover 82,000 sq ft across six floors, electric heating via air-source heat pumps, Swedish deli/restaurant, “As-Is” secondhand section, and no-car-park urban accessibility angle. Ends with CTA: “Share your learnings from your own city-format pilots.” --- #### Ch2 (PRIMARY) — People & Belonging: Tack! + Culture (3 items) **Item 1 (Sticky):** - Title: “Tack! in practice: how co-workers say thanks, in the IKEA way” - Type: guide - tags: ['tack', 'culture', 'recognition', 'togetherness'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/community.png **Body outline:** Explain Tack! concept with practical examples: recognition tied to outcomes and everyday behaviours; encourage short notes. Ends with “Write one co-worker thanks today.” **Item 2:** - Title: “One IKEA Bonus: unit-level performance, shared goals, shared wins” - Type: guide - tags: ['bonus', 'performance', 'co-worker', 'ingka-bonus'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/QualityCheck.png **Body outline:** Describe the unit-level approach and why it reinforces team-over-individual. Ends with CTA to ask your local leader for unit-goal details. **Item 3 (Highlight):** - Title: “Hybrid by design: why we supported flexible schedules to improve wellbeing” - Type: recap - tags: ['hybrid', 'wellbeing', 'learning', 'people'] - is_main_highlight: **True** - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/Thinking.png **Body outline:** Ground it in profile reality: offices hybrid, retail/logistics fully on-site. Tie to retention and wellbeing (without inventing new numbers). Ends with “If your role changes, check the practical guidance in your region.” --- #### Ch3 (PRIMARY) — Democratic Design: Product, Price & Innovation (3 items) **Item 1 (Sticky + Highlight):** - Title: “Prices down again: FY24–FY25 price reductions and what it means for the many people” - Type: announcement - tags: ['price-reduction', 'democratic-design', 'fy24', 'fy25'] - is_main_highlight: **True** - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/financial_report.png **Body outline:** Use profile facts: >€2.1B price reductions in FY24; 5.5% revenue decline in FY24; FY25 continued lowering prices (Ingka revenue -0.9% to €41.5B) while quantities grew 1.6%; net profit +75% to €1.4B. Ends with CTA: “Read local store notes for what’s changing at your meeting points.” **Item 2:** - Title: “IKEA second-hand marketplace (Sweden, Jan 2026): circularity at scale” - Type: spotlight - tags: ['circularity', 'second-hand', 'sweden', '2026'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/DigitalContent.png **Body outline:** Cover launch in Sweden in Jan 2026; plans to expand to Spain, Norway, Portugal, Poland; connect to “As-Is” sections. Ends with “Help customers find pre-loved pieces responsibly.” **Item 3:** - Title: “Best Buy shop-in-shop (US): the pilot that brings IKEA closer” - Type: recap - tags: ['best-buy', 'shop-in-shop', 'us-strategy', 'access'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: False - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/Idea.png **Body outline:** Explain US pilot (2025–2026), IKEA sections inside select Best Buy stores, reciprocally Best Buy consultation spaces inside IKEA stores; mention it as response to accessibility barriers away from large formats. Ends with CTA for co-workers in pilot markets to share learnings. --- #### Ch4 (SECONDARY) — Security & IT Safety (2 items, both require read confirmation) **Item 1 (Sticky):** - Title: “Account safety essentials: password hygiene and phishing response, in plain steps” - Type: policy - tags: ['security', 'passwords', 'phishing', 'it-safety'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: True - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/security.png **Body outline:** Give practical steps: stop-click-report workflow, approved systems only, hybrid working caution. Ends: “Confirm you’ve read it to keep moving.” **Item 2:** - Title: “Hybrid data handling: what not to store locally, and why it matters” - Type: guide - tags: ['hybrid', 'data-protection', 'security', 'best-practice'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: True - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/DataMaintenance.png **Body outline:** Explain approved storage/access, minimize local copies, follow security lifecycle. Ends with “If you’re unsure, ask IT before you save.” --- #### Ch5 (SECONDARY) — Policies & Compliance Acknowledgements (2 items, both require read confirmation) **Item 1 (Sticky):** - Title: “Reorganisation read-confirmation: co-worker responsibilities during restructuring” - Type: policy - tags: ['compliance', 'restructuring', 'co-worker', 'process'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: True - require_read_confirmation: True - status: Published - notify: True - image_url: stock/agreement.png **Body outline:** Tie to profile facts: Ingka elimination ~800 Group Functions roles (625 in Sweden) announced March 2026; Inter IKEA job cuts 850 (~3%, 300 in Sweden/Älmhult) announced May 2026; state purpose: ensure shared process knowledge. Ends: “Acknowledge to confirm you understand the required next steps.” **Item 2:** - Title: “Circularity & responsible operations: acknowledging updated expectations for ‘As-Is’ and second-hand support” - Type: policy - tags: ['sustainability', 'compliance', 'circularity', 'operations'] - is_main_highlight: False - is_sticky: False - require_read_confirmation: True - status: Published - notify: False - image_url: stock/sustainability.png **Body outline:** Ground in known initiatives: Sweden marketplace Jan 2026 and “As-Is” already in many stores; describe compliance-oriented expectations (handling, customer communications, responsible operations). Ends: “Confirm you’ve read the guidance before supporting customers.” --- ### is_main_highlight total check (exactly 3, within requirement 3–4) Set to **True** on: 1) “FY25 in numbers: Ingka retail €41.5B revenue, net profit €1.4B (+75%)” 2) “Hybrid by design: why we supported flexible schedules to improve wellbeing” 3) “Prices down again: FY24–FY25 price reductions and what it means for the many people” --- ### 4) HUB-LEVEL POLISH (Settings) **company_name:** IKEA **website_name:** IKEA (Intranet) *(not an external URL; just display naming)* **default_language:** en-US (HQ context: Netherlands/Sweden; IKEA uses English as business common language; profile doesn’t specify HQ country codes—if you prefer fi/se/sv/da, tell me which.) **supported_languages:** infer 11+ languages mentioned; set a representative list from profile language practice (English + 10 others) is not explicitly enumerated. ➡️ To avoid inventing language tags, I will need your confirmation: **should I set supported_languages to just ['en-US']** for now, or do you want specific tags listed? (I can only use tags you confirm or we fetch from somewhere else—no tool for that.) **login_quick_links (3–5 real URLs from profile):** - https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ - https://about.ikea.com - https://www.ingka.com/newsroom/ - https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/newsroom/ - https://www.ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/ **sidebar_navigation (ordered, IKEA priorities):** 1) collaborators *(people-first culture, co-workers + Tack!)* 2) workgroups *(Democratic Design/product pipeline teams)* 3) learning *(onboarding + ongoing training platform)* 4) documents *(policies & compliance)* 5) approvalRequests *(acknowledgements/workflows)* --- ## Confirmation question (required before any tool calls) This is a complete design. Shall I build it end-to-end, or would you like to adjust anything first—**channel names**, **content plan**, **colors**, or **hub settings** (especially supported_languages + the “IKEA Inside” topbar newsroom link)?

arguments.name: IKEA Intranet (Co-workers)
arguments.description: An intranet for IKEA co-workers to find practical, timely information—business updates, people & culture, and policy guidance—written in the IKEA way.
arguments.welcome_message: Welcome, co-worker. Here you’ll find “IKEA Inside” updates, practical policies you can rely on, and the things you need to do your best work—with a little less complexity and a little more clarity.

channels.name: IKEA Inside: Co-worker Updates
channels.description:
For the things co-workers need to know—quickly and clearly. IKEA Inside items connect performance, product launches, and real-world decisions.

You’ll find pinned context first, then the details you can act on.

frequent_questions.question: Where do I find the latest leadership transitions and what changed?
frequent_questions.answer: Use this channel for CEO and Group changes. Look for items pinned to the top first—then read the linked context sections.

frequent_questions.question: How do I interpret price reductions vs profit changes?
frequent_questions.answer: We share the “what” and the “why” in plain language: FY24–FY25 price reductions, revenue movement, and what recovered in net profit.

frequent_questions.question: Are updates global or country-specific?
frequent_questions.answer: These are global updates. Local implications are shared by your region/store leadership in the same week.

channels.name: People & Belonging: Tack! + Culture
channels.description:
Culture at IKEA isn’t a poster—it’s the way we solve problems together. This channel brings you the practical parts of “the IKEA way”: people programmes, learning, and wellbeing.

Look for pinned posts when something affects how we work.

frequent_questions.question: What is Tack! and how do I participate?
frequent_questions.answer: Tack! is the internal thanks programme. Share a real “thank you” tied to something co-workers actually did.

frequent_questions.question: How does hybrid work work here?
frequent_questions.answer: Office co-workers operate on hybrid schedules. If your role requires on-site coverage, your local manager will clarify the model.

frequent_questions.question: Where do learning opportunities live?
frequent_questions.answer: Use the learning-related updates and link out to the internal training platform details published in this channel.

channels.name: Democratic Design: Product, Price & Innovation
channels.description:
From Älmhult designers to store shelves: this is where we explain the “why” behind products and launches. You’ll see how we balance form, function, quality, sustainability, and low price.

When we make price reductions or launch a new format, this channel gives you the context.

frequent_questions.question: What does Democratic Design mean for pricing?
frequent_questions.answer: Design starts with the price target. If affordability fails, the product fails—no matter how elegant it looks.

frequent_questions.question: Where do second-hand and circular initiatives fit?
frequent_questions.answer: They’re part of the sustainability work and also capture value from the secondary market—starting with Sweden plans.

frequent_questions.question: How do city-format stores change what we do?
frequent_questions.answer: City formats change formats, assortment, and planning services—faster and more cost-efficient, while keeping co-worker and customer experience in focus.

channels.name: Security & IT Safety
channels.description:
Security is not optional here. This channel contains IT safety guidance and policy items you must acknowledge before continuing with specific internal workflows.

Read carefully—short, clear steps beat long debates.

frequent_questions.question: What do I do if I suspect a phishing message?
frequent_questions.answer: Stop, do not click. Report it using your local IT reporting channel (follow the steps in this hub’s pinned Security items).

frequent_questions.question: How should I handle sensitive files when working hybrid?
frequent_questions.answer: Use approved systems only and avoid storing sensitive data locally. This keeps access controlled and traceable.

frequent_questions.question: Why are password practices so strict?
frequent_questions.answer: Because account compromise is preventable. Password hygiene is a shared protection, not “just IT rules.”

channels.name: Policies & Compliance Acknowledgements
channels.description:
Policy items live here with clear effective dates and co-worker-readable language. When you see a policy with “read confirmation,” that means acknowledgement is required.

We keep the structure simple: read, confirm, and move on.

frequent_questions.question: Do I need to acknowledge policy updates every time?
frequent_questions.answer: Only items marked as required acknowledgements in this channel. Those will include ‘read confirmation’.

frequent_questions.question: Which policies relate to working models and reorganisations?
frequent_questions.answer: We publish the relevant policy consequences alongside restructure updates—so co-workers know what applies where.

frequent_questions.question: Where can I find the official rationale and effective dates?
frequent_questions.answer: Each policy item includes an effective date and a short rationale so you can understand the “why,” not just the “what.”

arguments.name: IKEA Intranet (Co-workers)
arguments.description: An intranet for IKEA co-workers to find practical, timely information, business updates, people and culture, and policy guidance written in the IKEA way.
arguments.welcome_message: Welcome, co-worker. Here you will find IKEA Inside updates, practical policies you can rely on, and the things you need to do your best work with a little less complexity and a little more clarity.

links.caption: IKEA Foundation climate and childrens futures

channels.name: IKEA Inside Co-worker Updates
channels.description:
For the things co-workers need to know quickly and clearly. IKEA Inside items connect performance, product launches, and real-world decisions.

You will find pinned context first, then the details you can act on.

frequent_questions.question: Where do I find the latest leadership transitions and what changed
frequent_questions.answer: Use this channel for CEO and Group changes. Look for items pinned to the top first, then read the linked context sections.

frequent_questions.question: How do I interpret price reductions vs profit changes
frequent_questions.answer: We share the what and the why in plain language: FY24-FY25 price reductions, revenue movement, and what recovered in net profit.

frequent_questions.question: Are updates global or country specific
frequent_questions.answer: These are global updates. Local implications are shared by your region or store leadership in the same week.

channels.name: People and Belonging Tack and Culture
channels.description:
Culture at IKEA is not a poster, it is the way we solve problems together. This channel brings you the practical parts of the IKEA way: people programmes, learning, and wellbeing.

Look for pinned posts when something affects how we work.

frequent_questions.question: What is Tack and how do I participate
frequent_questions.answer: Tack is the internal thanks programme. Share a real thank you tied to something co-workers actually did.

frequent_questions.question: How does hybrid work work here
frequent_questions.answer: Office co-workers operate on hybrid schedules. If your role requires on-site coverage, your local manager will clarify the model.

frequent_questions.question: Where do learning opportunities live
frequent_questions.answer: Use the learning related updates and link out to the internal training platform details published in this channel.

channels.name: Democratic Design Product Price and Innovation
channels.description:
From Älmhult designers to store shelves, this is where we explain the why behind products and launches. You will see how we balance form, function, quality, sustainability, and low price.

When we make price reductions or launch a new format, this channel gives you the context.

frequent_questions.question: What does Democratic Design mean for pricing
frequent_questions.answer: Design starts with the price target. If affordability fails, the product fails, no matter how elegant it looks.

frequent_questions.question: Where do second-hand and circular initiatives fit
frequent_questions.answer: They are part of the sustainability work and also capture value from the secondary market, starting with Sweden plans.

frequent_questions.question: How do city-format stores change what we do
frequent_questions.answer: City formats change formats, assortment, and planning services, faster and more cost efficient, while keeping co-worker and customer experience in focus.

channels.name: Security and IT Safety
channels.description:
Security is not optional here. This channel contains IT safety guidance and policy items you must acknowledge before continuing with specific internal workflows.

Read carefully, short clear steps beat long debates.

frequent_questions.question: What do I do if I suspect a phishing message
frequent_questions.answer: Stop, do not click. Report it using your local IT reporting channel, follow the steps in this hub pinned Security items.

frequent_questions.question: How should I handle sensitive files when working hybrid
frequent_questions.answer: Use approved systems only and avoid storing sensitive data locally. This keeps access controlled and traceable.

frequent_questions.question: Why are password practices so strict
frequent_questions.answer: Because account compromise is preventable. Password hygiene is a shared protection, not just IT rules.

channels.name: Policies and Compliance Acknowledgements
channels.description:
Policy items live here with clear effective dates and co-worker readable language. When you see a policy with read confirmation, that means acknowledgement is required.

We keep the structure simple: read, confirm, and move on.

frequent_questions.question: Do I need to acknowledge policy updates every time
frequent_questions.answer: Only items marked as required acknowledgements in this channel. Those will include read confirmation.

frequent_questions.question: Which policies relate to working models and reorganisations
frequent_questions.answer: We publish the relevant policy consequences alongside restructure updates so co-workers know what applies where.

frequent_questions.question: Where can I find the official rationale and effective dates
frequent_questions.answer: Each policy item includes an effective date and a short rationale so you can understand the why, not just the what.
